content.text: Community members expressed concerns about ElizaOS token performance reaching new all-time lows and questioned project direction. Complaints included lack of marketing, inactive Discord, and delayed product shipping. Core Dev Odilitime responded that the team remains committed to building, working on airdrops for holders, and promoting builders. He acknowledged market conditions are down and FUD is up but emphasized continued work on the Spartan Degen AI agent and Cloud platform.

Odilitime addressed late token migration requests from users who missed the 90-day deadline for migrating from ai16z to elizaOS tokens. He indicated plans to compile a list of late migration requests, noting complications because some users had already sold their ai16z tokens. The team took a snapshot and holds all ai16z from the migration, with transactions visible on-chain. Odilitime confirmed Solana and BSC are the two active chains.

Community members discussed Ruby token activity, which saw a 65 percent price increase. Odilitime clarified that Ruby is not a Labs project and there are no official plans to develop it, despite Shaw owning the Ruby IP. There was also confusion about which Milady token is legitimate, with Odilitime confirming there is no legitimate Milady token yet, though he mentioned working on a Milady project.

Discussion emerged about ElizaOS competitive position in the decentralized AI space. Community members noted Venice was integrated as a default LLM API option during OpenClaw installation, suggesting strategic partnerships. Some members expressed mixed sentiment about the main token performance, noting competition from other AI projects like Venice, Morpheus, and Gonka that have attracted investor attention with different tokenomics models.

Venice VVV market performance was discussed, noting it took off with over 1 million users. The project implemented a strategic tokenomics model where 50 percent of supply was airdropped to Base and AI community addresses. Users who stake tokens receive free inference compute, with 30 percent of holders staking while 70 percent of network capacity remains available for commercial sales. The model uses a freemium approach where stakers get proportional access to compute resources.

Odilitime shared a full conversation interview with Shaw, Founder of elizaOS, covering key topics including why models decide agent usability, agent benchmarks shifting into games, how simulation data builds trading competence, tokens turning ideas into dev budgets fast, and why reinforcement learning made models improve rapidly. ElizaBAO shared a video snippet from Wise Ones Episode 6 featuring Shaw discussing the emotional challenge of watching 25 million dollars drop to 120K without selling.

Community members discussed Aivilization, an open-world simulation game for digital lives featuring AI agents that can live, work, socialize, fight, and post their own thoughts publicly. The concept generated interest, with suggestions that it could capture a large audience. It was noted that Cayden was working on elizatown, a similar concept.

Jin released Cron Job, a weekly AI-produced open-source ecosystem news show covering ElizaOS updates from GitHub and Discord. The first episodes are available on YouTube and M3TV. The show is built with a system designed to work for any GitHub and Discord community, with plans to add a segment covering development updates.

Developer Meme Broker released three new ElizaOS plugins. The plugin-heartbeat creates a scheduled recurring task system with quiet hours, error budgets, and dynamic task management. The plugin-mem0 integrates MEM0 for persistent long-term memory via API with auto-recall, auto-capture, and remember/recall/forget functions, working as a self-updating RAG system. The plugin-skill-loader converts OpenClaw SKILL.md files into live ElizaOS plugins with parser, converter, runtime loader, and standalone plugin generator capabilities.

APEX Oracle released version 0.5.0 as a deep-market analytics layer for Solana trading agents. The system includes Organic Absorption Ratio to detect wash-trading versus external liquidity, Funding DNA Analysis to trace ancestor wallets and identify Sybil farms, and Jito/MEV Toxicity monitoring for slot density and sandwich attack risks. The team developed a TypeScript wrapper for ElizaOS integration via APEX_TOKEN_SCAN action.

Jason from xproof.app announced PR 266 in the plugin registry, which adds on-chain audit trails for ElizaOS agents to certify decisions before execution with built-in compliance gates. The PR was approved by CodeRabbit and ready for maintainer review.
LillAnders introduced ZARQ, a crypto risk intelligence infrastructure for AI agents, and published an ElizaOS plugin providing pre-trade risk scoring for 205 tokens.

N0vaMp4 introduced a concept for an enforcement primitive for agent-to-vendor credit lines, where agent operators post bonds and vendors get atomic slashing rights if agents default on payments. The developer sought validation on whether unpaid compute from agents exhausting balances mid-task is a real problem for API and service providers.

The ElizaOS team made significant progress on documentation and project management. Sayonara shared auto-generated documentation at elizaos-eliza.mintlify.app/introduction, describing it as an open-source framework for building multi-agent AI applications that are extensible, production-ready, and model-agnostic. Odilitime reported cleaning up pull requests, reducing them to one page and adding proper labels.
A contributor submitted their first pull request to the elizaOS repository, fixing documentation in the readme, which was positively received by the community.

Community member satsbased announced an initiative to support builders creating projects on Eliza technology, offering to serve as an advisor and encouraging developers to make announcements in designated channels to receive community amplification from day one.

Nikita from Coin Post Media, a crypto media outlet with over 2 million followers across Telegram, YouTube, and X, reached out in the coders channel expressing interest in a potential collaboration with ElizaOS.

Stan shared progress on building a universal autoscaling solution for agents on the cloud that supports WhatsApp, Telegram, SMS, and all clients out of the box, with infrastructure that automatically scales agents based on demand.
